fix.description: Ensure parallel PR-Check stages (e.g. Build and Branch Check) do not share the same Gradle user home directory. Set GRADLE_USER_HOME to a unique per-build path (e.g. /data/gradle-${BUILD_NUMBER}) or add --gradle-user-home flag, or serialize the stages so they do not run concurrently on the same agent.

rag_query: Android Gradle build fails Timeout waiting to lock journal cache journal-1.lock currently in use by another Gradle instance parallel builds shared cache directory lock contention CI
ground_truth_rca: The PR-Check pipeline runs two stages in parallel on the same agent: the main Build stage and the Branch Check stage. Both stages invoke Gradle with --no-daemon, which spins up separate single-use Gradle JVM processes that attempt to acquire an exclusive lock on the shared Gradle journal cache at /data/gradle-8.5/caches/journal-1/journal-1.lock. Because the first Gradle process (PID from Build stage) was still holding the lock when the Branch Check stage started, the second process timed out after 1m 2s and the Branch Check and Sonar stages both failed. The main build artifact (APK) was produced successfully in the prior stage. The Jenkins pipeline reports Finished: SUCCESS because all failures are wrapped in catchError blocks and the post-build PR approval step completed successfully — another instance of the false-positive pipeline status pattern.

rag_query: iOS fastlane upload_file lane fails after successful IPA upload with undefined method for nil JSON parse error distribution API returns empty response
ground_truth_rca: Build, archive, and export all succeeded (including enterprise signing and dSYM compression). The IPA (174MB) was uploaded to the distribution server via curl (100% transfer at ~5MB/s, 34 seconds). However, the distribution API returned an unexpected response — when Fastfile:157 attempted to access response_json[0]['documentId'], the parsed response was nil, causing a NoMethodError. This indicates the distribution server accepted the upload but returned an empty or malformed response body instead of the expected JSON array. Root cause is server-side: either the distribution service failed to process the uploaded artifact, or a transient server error occurred after receiving the file. The 13 SwiftFormat build phase failures and CFBundleVersion mismatch warning (app extension version 10002 vs parent app 79) are cosmetic issues that did not prevent the build.

The workspace has @2 suffix indicating a concurrent build which may compound network pressure. Fix: increase git timeout in Jenkins (checkout timeout > 10 min), retry the build, or configure shallow clone (--depth=1) for deployment pipelines to reduce transfer size. Also investigate network stability between iosagent-1 and Bitbucket server.
